{"step_by_step_thinking": "Intraoperative histopathological analysis provides a definitive diagnosis of the tumor, which means it gives a definitive diagnosis. It is used for detecting positive margins after resection, confirming suspected metastasis, and sentinel lymph node biopsy in breast carcinoma is an example of this process. Therefore, the statement that is not true is 'Gives an immediate definitive diagnosis of tumor', as it implies that the diagnosis is immediate, which is not necessarily the case as the process can take some time.", "answer_choice": "A"}
